name: remember-skill
context_prose: E13 remember skill captures explicit knowledge the user dictates ("remember this", "remember that", "don't forget", `/remember`) as feedback or fact memories with user approval. Unlike E11 learn skill, which reviews session work for implicit lessons, remember handles user-initiated saves: the user names what to capture and the skill walks the agent through classification, a three-gate quality check (Recurs, Actionable, Right home), drafting with a `/prepare`-shaped situation field, and persistence via `engram learn` (or `engram update` on dedup hit). The skill body is markdown text loaded by Claude Code (E3) on slash-command or trigger-phrase match (R1); the agent then shells out to the engram CLI binary (E9) per R5. The skill performs no I/O itself — it is text instructing the agent — so all properties below are textual invariants of SKILL.md, enforced by the prose at the cited line and validated only by reading the document.

edges.label: loads skill body on /remember or trigger phrase
instructs agent: engram learn / engram update

properties.name: Trigger phrases routed
properties.statement: For all user inputs containing "remember this", "remember that", "don't forget", "save this for later", or `/remember`, Claude Code loads the remember skill body as the next agent message.
properties.notes: Enforced by the frontmatter description; matched by Claude Code's skill router (E3 → R1). No automated test in this repo.

properties.name: Five-step flow ordering
properties.statement: For all candidate items the user wants saved, the agent executes the flow as Step 1 Classify → Step 2 Quality gate → Step 3 Draft and present → Step 4 Save → Step 5 Handle results, in that order.
properties.notes: Section ordering is the contract; no skill-runner asserts traversal.

properties.name: Classify into feedback or fact
properties.statement: For all candidates, Step 1 classifies each as either a Feedback memory (situation → behavior → impact → action) or a Fact memory (situation → subject → predicate → object); a single user request may yield multiple memories.
properties.notes: The two memory shapes correspond to the `engram learn feedback` and `engram learn fact` subcommands invoked in Step 4.

properties.name: Quality gates ordered, fail-fast
properties.statement: For all candidates, the three quality gates are checked in order — (1) Recurs, (2) Actionable, (3) Right home — and a single failure drops the candidate; the agent tells the user why and suggests the right home instead.
properties.notes: Short-circuit semantics: gate ordering matters because Recurs failures are the most common and cheapest to detect.

properties.name: Recurs gate strips to activity+domain
properties.statement: For all candidates, the Recurs gate fails if the situation, after stripping to "activity + domain", names this project (engram / traced / etc.), its internals or architecture, phase numbers, issue IDs, commit hashes, dates, one-time events, diary entries, or status snapshots.
properties.notes: Pass criterion: an agent working on an unrelated web app, game, or data pipeline should plausibly hit the same situation.

properties.name: Actionable gate requires concrete action
properties.statement: For all candidates, the Actionable gate fails on vague observations ("things can go wrong"), inert facts ("X exists"), or raw debug logs; a passing memory names a concrete action that changes what an agent would DO.

properties.name: Right-home alternative named
properties.statement: For all candidates that reach the Right-home gate, the agent first names the alternative home — one of: code, a doc, a skill, CLAUDE.md, a `.claude/rules/*.md` file, or a spec/plan under `docs/`.
properties.notes: Naming the alternative is mandatory before verification; prevents the gate from defaulting to memory.

properties.name: Right-home verification via git log
properties.statement: For all Right-home checks, the agent verifies the claimed home by running `git log --since='14 days ago' --name-only --pretty=format: -- docs/ specs/ plans/ skills/ CLAUDE.md .claude/`, then reads the listed files and greps for the candidate's content.
properties.notes: 14-day window scopes verification to recently-touched files; sort -u dedupes paths.

properties.name: Verification outcome trichotomy
properties.statement: For all Right-home verifications, exactly one of three outcomes is taken: (a) home contains it AND surfaced in time → Move on; (b) home contains it BUT did not surface → Ask user (reinforce vs note); (c) home lacks it or no home fits → Ask user (save anyway?).
properties.notes: Reading the home during verification does not count as surfacing — surfacing must come from /recall, /prepare, or CLAUDE.md auto-load earlier in the session.

properties.name: Reinforce path uses engram update on duplicate
properties.statement: For all reinforce-path persists where `engram learn` returns DUPLICATE, the agent broadens the existing memory's situation via `engram update --name ... --situation "..."` rather than dismissing the duplicate.
properties.notes: Duplicate is treated as a surfacing failure: the memory exists but did not reach the agent in time.

properties.name: Situation framed as activity+domain
properties.statement: For all surviving candidates, the drafted Situation field describes the task the agent would be embarking on as activity + domain — not the diagnosis, symptom, or fix — matching how `/prepare` queries would be phrased BEFORE the lesson is known.
properties.notes: Bad: "When fixing context cancellation in concurrent code" (bakes in hindsight). Good: "When writing concurrent Go code with context".

properties.name: User approval before save
properties.statement: For all surviving candidates, the agent drafts all fields and presents them for user approval before issuing any `engram learn` or `engram update` command in Step 4.
properties.notes: Defining property of remember vs learn — the user opted in by name; approval confirms the drafted shape.

properties.name: Persistence delegated to engram CLI
properties.statement: For all approved candidates, persistence is performed by shelling out to `engram learn feedback --situation ... --behavior ... --impact ... --action ... --source human` (feedback) or `engram learn fact --situation ... --subject ... --predicate ... --object ... --source human` (fact); the skill itself performs no file or network I/O.
properties.notes: Realises R5 (remember skill → engram CLI binary) at the per-invocation level.

properties.name: Source flag pinned to human
properties.statement: For all `engram learn` invocations issued from Step 4, the `--source` flag equals `human` — distinguishing user-dictated remember saves from agent-derived learn saves.
properties.notes: Lets downstream analytics weight human-sourced memories differently from machine-derived ones.

properties.name: Result handling trichotomy
properties.statement: For all `engram learn` results, the agent handles exactly one of: CREATED → confirm to user; DUPLICATE → diagnose why /recall or /prepare missed it and broaden via `engram update` (never dismiss); CONTRADICTION → present the conflict and ask the user to update, replace, or keep both.
properties.notes: Matches the three result codes the engram CLI's learn pipeline can return.

properties.name: Duplicate diagnosed as surfacing failure
properties.statement: For all DUPLICATE results, the agent treats the outcome as a surfacing failure (recall/prepare missed an existing memory), diagnoses why, and broadens the existing memory's situation rather than dismissing the duplicate.
properties.notes: Tightly coupled to P10; this property is the result-handling phrasing, P10 is the verify-phase phrasing.

properties.name: Contradiction defers to user
properties.statement: For all CONTRADICTION results, the agent presents the conflict to the user and asks whether to update existing, replace, or keep both — never auto-resolves.
properties.notes: User holds final authority on conflicting beliefs; agent is the messenger.
